overview_faqs.question: How much is the total BTech fee at Jagannath University Haryana?
overview_faqs.answer: The total BTech fee in Jagannath University Haryana comes around INR 3.56 L to 5.4 L approximately The fee can be more or less depending on which specialisation student is taking. Also, tuition fee is generally paid at start of academic year. Apart from tuition fee, the total fee may include hostel charges, lab charges or other miscallenous charges.

overview_faqs.question: Is pursuing MBA at Jagannath University Haryana expensive?
overview_faqs.answer: If you belong to an upper-middle-class family then the expenses are not too much for you but if you belong to a lower-middle-class family then it can be a bit expensive for you. The first-year fee for MBA is approximately INR 1.15 lac. Also, if you are a residence of another state, then it can be really expensive for you. As you can't get a scholarship from your state. Although you can take scholarships offered by central organizations. But there are many criteria for that.Hope you find it helpful.

overview_faqs.question: What is the fees for various courses at Jagannath University Haryana?
overview_faqs.answer: As it is a private university fees of this university may be higher as compared to several Government university.For any bachelor/undergraduate programs like B.Tech, BBA, BCA, etc. the fees ranges from Rs.60,000 to Rs.1,20,000 and for master degree programme like M.Tech, MBA, MCA, etc. the fees ranges from Rs.80,000 to Rs.2,00000. It is the approximate fees of these course, You are adviced to contact with the college reception to get the exact fees structure of the different courses.The fees for professional courses like B.Tech, MBA tend to be higher than general degree courses, and there is a hike in fees of this university every year. If any students need to opt any hostel and mess facilitues they have to pay extra for that as they state to pay them.

overview_faqs.question: How good is Jagannath University Haryana?
overview_faqs.answer: As per the NIRF report, Jagannath University placement percentage is between 75-80%. Jagannath University has been accredited with Grade 'B' by the National Assessment and Accreditation Council and is also a member of the Association of Indian Universities. The eligibility criteria for admission to this University is 10+2 examination with Physics and Mathematics as compulsory subjects along with one of the Chemistry /Biotechnology / Biology / Technical Vocational subjects. Students must obtain at least 45% marks (40% in the case of candidates belonging to the SC/ST category) in the above subjects taken together.The average salary package offered here is 5.50 Lakhs to 4.80 Lakhs per annum. Top recruiters are - Kotak Mahindra Bank, Development Consultant, Arkiplan International etc.

review_summary.college_name: Jagannath University Reviews on Placements, Faculty & Facilities

individual_reviews.course: B.Tech. in Computer Science and Engineering - Batch of 2027
individual_reviews.title: The college infra and all things are good but canteen and hostel food is below average

content.Placements: The placement is average. The highest package is 14 lakh, the lowest package is of 3 lakh and the average package in this university is 4 to 6 lakh. Also paid or unpaid internships are offered that are held by the university or if anyone want to outside from campus they can also try that.
content.Infrastructure: The infra is good and labs are average in this university. There is free unlimited Wi-Fi, modern labs and a library in which we can issue books to use.
content.Faculty: Faculty is much better than other departments. They are supportive and their teaching is good. They use different methods and we all have to study in a good manner. All the faculty members are well qualified. The semester exams are not that hard and the pass percentage is 40.

individual_reviews.course: Bachelor of Pharmacy (B.Pharm.) - Batch of 2024
individual_reviews.title: A bachelor's degree in my life.

content.Placements: Students who score above 60% and get marks in the first division are placed easily. Students get hired by companies like Dr. JRK Research, Belco Pharma, etc. The highest package ranges from 3-4 LPA and the lowest package ranges from 1.5-2 LPA. Internships are provided by Arbro Pharma and ACPL.
content.Infrastructure: The infrastructure is good. It has a Wi-Fi campus, AC classrooms, smart classes, and well-furnished labs for every subject. The canteen and Nestled cafeteria are provided on the campus. Annual sports are also organised at the university level every year.

individual_reviews.course: Bachelor of Physiotherapy (BPT) - Batch of 2026
individual_reviews.title: The education is good, but the infrastructure could be more good.

content.Placements: They give choices to kids to either get a placement on their own based on their personal choices or to be provide some hospital for an internship. Top recruiting companies, such as Adhar Hospital. About 80% of students get internships from the university as general physiotherapists.
content.Infrastructure: In my BPT course, we design machines related to our course, along with an OPD and labs for different subjects, such as the exercise therapy lab and anatomy lab. The college provides well-equipped hostels for both girls and boys, and there is a mess that provides three meals a day and snacks in the evening. The university annually organizes a sports meet, which lasts for three continuous days for intra-university students, along with various games.

individual_reviews.course: B.Tech. in Computer Science and Engineering - Batch of 2027
individual_reviews.title: Jagannath - The best college.

content.Placements: Jagannath University has a collaboration with the Bosch and Bridge. They provide us with placements in good companies where we get good salaries and workloads are also reliable and less. The average package of college in B.Tech is around 3 LPA.
content.Infrastructure: College infrastructure is very good and reliable for both students and teachers. Our faculty members of Jagannath are very helpful and friendly with us. They help us a lot in our studies and even give us extra classes. I didn't opt for the hostel.

individual_reviews.course: Integrated B.A. + B.Ed. - Batch of 2026
individual_reviews.title: Fair exams and studies, but dissatisfied because of no activities and opportunities.

content.Placements: The University regularly organizes placement drives, offering opportunities in various sectors like IT, management, engineering, and more. But not all courses get these opportunities. In my course, they don't even provide us with schools for internships. We are just given the internship letter to go to a school of our choice.
content.Infrastructure: Wi-Fi is not strong. The labs are okay and properly equipped. Classrooms have ACs that never work. The library never has the books needed. They neither have novels nor course-related books. Hostel food is okay. No AC in the hostels. Recently, they put coolers after so many complaints from students.

courses_data.description: Jagannath University offers a Full Time BE/BTech that lasts 4 years. To get into this programme, students need to have good scores in the JEE Main exam. Below is more information about the eligibility requirements, selection criteria, and fees for Jagannath University BE/BTech admission in 2025.

faqs.question: When will CAT results be announced for MBA admission?
faqs.answer: Common Admission is a national-level MBA entrance exam to get admission into Indian B-Schools. CAT registration takes place in August and it ends in September. Usually, admit cards are released in October and the exam takes place in November every year. Results are declared by January. Based on CAT results, students can apply to multiple B-Schools.

faqs.question: When will Jagannath University cutoff release?
faqs.answer: Jagannath University will release its admission cutoff based on the entrance examination it conducts, the Jagan Nath University Entrance Test (JUEE) or the Common University Entrance Test (CUET), whichever is applicable for your program. The university will then release its own cutoff list on its official website.

faqs.question: How is BTech admission process in Jagannath University Haryana 2025?
faqs.answer: Jagannath University BTech admission process 2025 is simple. First you check eligibility that is if you have passed Class 12 with PCM and atleast 45%. Then, apply through JEE, CUET or JUEE or also directly based on merit. The merit scored in the qualifying exam is considered. After that, fill application form online to start the process. If selected, attend counselling and finish document verification in campus.

faqs.question: What is the selection criteria for MBA admission at Jagannath University, Haryana in 2025?
faqs.answer: Selection Criteria for MBA programme at Jagannath University, Haryana in 2025 is based on a candidate's performance in entrance exams such asCAT, MAT, XAT, CMAT, ATMA, orCUET-PG. Applicants must also meet theminimum eligibility requirement of 50% aggregate marks in their UG. Admissions are conducted primarily on afirst-come, first-served basis.So early application is advisable.

placement_report.college_name: Jagannath University Placement & Salary Insights

placement_data.title: Jagannath University Placement Overview 2023
paragraphs: The University has not released the 2023 placement statistics yet. However, the NIRF report 2023 has been released. As per the report, 218 UG and 84 PG students were placed during Jagannath University Bahadurgarh placements 2022. The median package offered to UG (4-year) students was INR 3.65 LPA. For UG (3-year) and U (5-year) courses, the median package stood at INR 3.05 LPA and INR 3.35 LPA , respectively. On the other hand, the median package for PG courses ranged between INR 3.70 LPA and INR 4.15 LPA during Jagannath University placements 2022 .

faqs.question: How much is the total BTech fee at Jagannath University Haryana?
faqs.answer: The total BTech fee in Jagannath University Haryana comes around INR 3.56 L to 5.4 L approximately The fee can be more or less depending on which specialisation student is taking. Also, tuition fee is generally paid at start of academic year. Apart from tuition fee, the total fee may include hostel charges, lab charges or other miscallenous charges.

faqs.question: Is pursuing MBA at Jagannath University Haryana expensive?
faqs.answer: If you belong to an upper-middle-class family then the expenses are not too much for you but if you belong to a lower-middle-class family then it can be a bit expensive for you. The first-year fee for MBA is approximately INR 1.15 lac. Also, if you are a residence of another state, then it can be really expensive for you. As you can't get a scholarship from your state. Although you can take scholarships offered by central organizations. But there are many criteria for that. Hope you find it helpful.

faqs.question: What is the fees for various courses at Jagannath University Haryana?
faqs.answer: As it is a private university fees of this university may be higher as compared to several Government university. For any bachelor/undergraduate programs like B.Tech, BBA, BCA, etc. the fees ranges from Rs.60,000 to Rs.1,20,000 and for master degree programme like M.Tech, MBA, MCA, etc. the fees ranges from Rs.80,000 to Rs.2,00000. It is the approximate fees of these course, You are adviced to contact with the college reception to get the exact fees structure of the different courses. The fees for professional courses like B.Tech, MBA tend to be higher than general degree courses, and there is a hike in fees of this university every year. If any students need to opt any hostel and mess facilitues they have to pay extra for that as they state to pay them.

faqs.question: What are extra charges included in BSc programme fee at Jagannath University, Haryana in 2025?
faqs.answer: The total Jagannath University Fee amount may include other charges, if applicable. A one-time admission payment is also charged by the university. Hostel charges if applicable is charged along with admission charges. Whereas, the total tuition fee is paid at the beginning of each academic year.

hostel_campus.college_name: Jagannath University Infrastructure Details & Reviews

hostel.description: The hostel rooms are spacious, well furnished and are provided with 24 hours internet facility, electricity and water supply. WI-FI facility for hostel residents is provided round the clock for easy access of information and studies. Gym and Mess facilities are also available in hostels.

library.description: Fully air-conditioned laboratory computerized using DELNET and MANUPATRA membership. The library has a special collection of prescribed textbooks called ‘Book Bank’. The library provides the latest research and reference material in print and audio-visual formats along with the facility to refer to e-journals, CDs, Project Reports, Government Publications, Reports and Newsletters. The library is being strengthened through digitization to provide access to MOOC and other online courses.

sports.description: Available Facilities: Badminton Court, Basketball Court, Cricket Ground, Football Ground, Tennis Court, Volley Ball Court, Table Tennis Room, Athletics Tracks, Hockey Field, Chess and Carom Room

reviews.review_heading: The college infra and all things are good but canteen and hostel food is below average
reviews.review_text: The infra is good and labs are average in this university. There is free unlimited Wi-Fi, modern labs and a library in which we can issue books to use.

reviews.review_heading: A bachelor's degree in my life.
reviews.review_text: The infrastructure is good. It has a Wi-Fi campus, AC classrooms, smart classes, and well-furnished labs for every subject. The canteen and Nestled cafeteria are provided on the campus. Annual sports are also organised at the university level every year.

reviews.review_heading: The education is good, but the infrastructure could be more good.
reviews.review_text: In my BPT course, we design machines related to our course, along with an OPD and labs for different subjects, such as the exercise therapy lab and anatomy lab. The college provides well-equipped hostels for both girls and boys, and there is a mess that provides three meals a day and snacks in the evening. The university annually organizes a sports meet, which lasts for three continuous days for intra-university students, along with various games.

reviews.review_heading: Jagannath - The best college.
reviews.review_text: College infrastructure is very good and reliable for both students and teachers. Our faculty members of Jagannath are very helpful and friendly with us. They help us a lot in our studies and even give us extra classes. I didn't opt for the hostel.

reviews.review_heading: Fair exams and studies, but dissatisfied because of no activities and opportunities.
reviews.review_text: Wi-Fi is not strong. The labs are okay and properly equipped. Classrooms have ACs that never work. The library never has the books needed. They neither have novels nor course-related books. Hostel food is okay. No AC in the hostels. Recently, they put coolers after so many complaints from students.

reviews.review_heading: JNU Jhajjar details about MPT course.
reviews.review_text: All facilities are available, including classrooms, laboratories, libraries, gyms, swimming pools, and Wi-Fi. The quality of the food in the mess is just okay, not great. Medical facilities are good; there are many labs for physiotherapy treatment. Sports and games are held annually there at the university.

reviews.review_heading: Life at Jagan Nath University.
reviews.review_text: The infrastructure of the university is large enough. There are different buildings for the different courses. The classrooms are also good for 80–85 students. Wi-Fi is available in every corner of the university. The library is on two floors and around 900–1200 books are available in the library. The hostel facility is also available at the university and there are different buildings for girls and boys hostels. The food is good and they give 4 meals a day to the students that live in the hoste...

reviews.review_heading: I am very satisfied with faculty members of this college.
reviews.review_text: Hostel rooms are clean and the environment is also good. The food provided by the hostel staff is quite impressive and tasty. Cleaners are provided to clean the rooms before the students get back to the hostel. Warden of the hostel is good and very friendly with students.

reviews.review_heading: It is fruitful for a UG student due to its moderate fee structure.
reviews.review_text: The infrastructure and facilities available on the campus are good. The campus has 3 labs and 1 library, and you can spend most of the time in the library and labs because students are allowed to avail it anytime. The college is far from Delhi, so it is very hectic for one to travel on a daily basis, but you can reside in the hostel provided.

reviews.review_heading: Review of Jagannath University Bahadurgarh.
reviews.review_text: Wi-Fi facility rarely worked on the campus, and we almost complained about it every day, and there was no response. Classrooms were good and well-designed, but the orientation of the structure is too bad. The campus is air-conditioned. Taking a journey for hours from Delhi to study is still a nightmare.

reviews.review_heading: I don't want to say anything.
reviews.review_text: No this university not provide us anything. They have a computer lab but without a computer they taking a designing classes but without a software also have a camera without a sensor. But faculty is too good no doubt on that part seriously they are the Best.

reviews.review_heading: Jagannath university is best for education.
reviews.review_text: Entire college is connected to a Wi-Fi network. All classrooms are fully air conditioned. And has also got a big sports ground with facility of all the sports and their is a gym also for fitness lovers. In the canteen the quality and taste of food is very good. And the hostels are very near to the university.

reviews.review_heading: Fully satisfied because of opportunities provided on-campus.
reviews.review_text: The campus is fully enabled with Wi-Fi. Labs are maintained properly as all the required labs for each department are available there. The library is always open for students, and even students have their library cards with which they can get books for some days. Quality food is served at the canteen. Infrastructure is very good.

reviews.review_heading: Once we were dissatisfied with them.
reviews.review_text: The infrastructure of campus is nice. They have two buildings in that they have separate department for course and cafeteria. Ground for volleyball. Basketball cricket n many more. They have a very nice hostel with good food n good facilities nice labs.

reviews.review_heading: Study is good in college.
reviews.review_text: All over building is okay. Classrooms are normal same as government colleges. Library is good. We get all the books which we required related to subjects. There is AC also in computer lab and library. There are two Computer lab. Quality is good. Canteen is not good. Only Small dairy products are there.

paragraphs: As it is a private university fees of this university may be higher as compared to several Government university.

For any bachelor/undergraduate programs like B.Tech, BBA, BCA, etc. the fees ranges from Rs.60,000 to Rs.1,20,000 and for master degree programme like M.Tech, MBA, MCA, etc. the fees ranges from Rs.80,000 to Rs.2,00000. It is the approximate fees of these course, You are adviced to contact with the college reception to get the exact fees structure of the different courses.

The fees for professional courses like B.Tech, MBA tend to be higher than general degree courses, and there is a hike in fees of this university every year. If any students need to opt any hostel and mess facilitues they have to pay extra for that as they state to pay them.
To encourage its bright students, Jagannath University offers various categories of scholarships.

The merit-based scholarships for BCom students at Jagannath University, Haryana are awarded based on the candidate's performance in the qualifying examination (Class 12). The university has a tier system:
Students securing above 90% marks in Class 12 receive a 35% tuition fee scholarship.
Those scoring between 80% and 90% are eligible for a 25% tuition fee concession.
Students with scores ranging from 70% to 80% can avail a 10% tuition fee scholarship.
Even those with marks between 60% and 70% are granted a 5% tuition fee concession.

Based on the information available on the university official website the university follows the UGC guideline to refund the fees to the students. It is mentioned in the terms and condition of the refund ploicy of the University "4.1. 3 If a student chooses to withdraw from the programme of study in which he/she is enrolled, the institution concerned shall follow the following five-tier system for the refund of fees* remitted by the student" .

reviews_list.review_title: It is a great college under Rajasthan Dental Association with highly-educated faculty.

review_content.Placements: The placement rate is above 60%. The highest package offered in BDS is 16 LPA. You can get a job across the world in the dental sector. Highly-educated professors conduct webinars here. All the games are available. Fresher's party is held every year. I recommend this college.
review_content.Infrastructure: Free Wi-Fi is available. The library is open till 8 pm. It has well-maintained labs and digital classes. Mess offers only vegetarian food. All the game contests are conducted.
review_content.Faculty: The teachers are top-ranked. Mr Ali sir, for endodontics, and our principal sir, Mr Bagwan Das, are highly skilled and highly educated.

reviews_list.batch_info: BDS - Bachelor of Dental Surgery - Batch of 2020
reviews_list.review_title: It is a good college for practice purposes, and you will know how to deal with all toes of patients.

review_content.Placements: There are 98 students in our batch. Overall college is good because of the OPD of patients but the education is also good. It is a good college to do an internship as you are allowed to go to all kinds of patients, but the disadvantage is that you are not given any stipend.
review_content.Infrastructure: The infrastructure of our college is pretty good and we have a central library with air-conditioners and departments with a good number of dental chairs. But there is no Wi-Fi facility and there are network issues with the college. The hostel rooms are good and well furnished with water coolers and aqua guards. The food served in the mess is edible.

content.Placements: The placement rate is above 60%. The highest package offered in BDS is 16 LPA. You can get a job across the world in the dental sector. Highly-educated professors conduct webinars here. All the games are available. Fresher's party is held every year. I recommend this college.
content.Infrastructure: Free Wi-Fi is available. The library is open till 8 pm. It has well-maintained labs and digital classes. Mess offers only vegetarian food. All the game contests are conducted.
content.Faculty: The teachers are top-ranked. Mr Ali sir, for endodontics, and our principal sir, Mr Bagwan Das, are highly skilled and highly educated.

individual_reviews.course: BDS - Bachelor of Dental Surgery - Batch of 2020
individual_reviews.title: It is a good college for practice purposes, and you will know how to deal with all toes of patients.

content.Placements: There are 98 students in our batch. Overall college is good because of the OPD of patients but the education is also good. It is a good college to do an internship as you are allowed to go to all kinds of patients, but the disadvantage is that you are not given any stipend.
content.Infrastructure: The infrastructure of our college is pretty good and we have a central library with air-conditioners and departments with a good number of dental chairs. But there is no Wi-Fi facility and there are network issues with the college. The hostel rooms are good and well furnished with water coolers and aqua guards. The food served in the mess is edible.

individual_reviews.course: BDS - Bachelor of Dental Surgery - Batch of 2021
individual_reviews.title: Looking forward for college to support in extracurricular activities.

content.Placements: Well we are the first batch here, but I am hoping for a good job after the course completion. All the students in private colleges related fields have to sign a bond in accordance to which we are bound to do internship from our college and if we wish to switch then there is a certain amount that needs to be paid.
content.Infrastructure: Wi-Fi facilities are provided in the hostels. Classrooms are well constructed with air-conditioners and proper sitting facilities along with projectors in the lecture halls. The library is big and spacious with all the required books. Labs are big enough to hold approximately 150 students. We have well maintained hostel rooms as well.

hostel_campus.college_name: Pacific Dental College and Hospital Infrastructure Details & Reviews

hostel.description: The college hostels have well-quipped amenities such as separate common rooms with color television and DTH connection, Visitor/parents waiting hall, medical facility, water purifiers, electrical generators, indoor and outdoor games facilities like table tennis, carrom, chess, basketball court, tennis court and cricket ground etc.

library.description: The college has well-quipped library with a collection of 5,000 books and 6,000 bound journal volumes date back to the mid-1900s and are international in scope. It holds over 11,000 volumes on dentistry, as well as the basic sciences and selected medical specialties.The central library of the college has been equipped with a separate fully air-conditioned reading hall, undergraduate and postgraduate sections, faculty reading area, a separate audio-visual room and a state of art computer lab with over 25 computers.It is equipped with Barcode Integration system to deal with the library management in the institution.It is subscribing more than 250 online journals and over 80 print journals. Wi-fi facility is also available. It provides access to more than 30 print magazines and newspapers for the benefit of students and faculty.

reviews.review_heading: It is a very good dental college in Rajasthan.
reviews.review_text: The library is well-equipped in our college. Air-conditioners are available. Our classrooms are well-furnished. The campus is not Wi-Fi-enabled. The infrastructure is good. Hostel facilities are also good.

reviews.review_heading: The college has good patient flow.
reviews.review_text: The hospitals are not as good as engineering colleges, but as I am at a private college, it has a good infrastructure with a vast library and 11 departments that are well maintained. Hostels are well-cleaned here. The mess is not far away, and so is the canteen.

reviews.review_heading: It is a great college under Rajasthan Dental Association with highly-educated faculty.
reviews.review_text: Free Wi-Fi is available. The library is open till 8 pm. It has well-maintained labs and digital classes. Mess offers only vegetarian food. All the game contests are conducted.

reviews.review_heading: It is a good college for practice purposes, and you will know how to deal with all toes of patients.
reviews.review_text: The infrastructure of our college is pretty good and we have a central library with air-conditioners and departments with a good number of dental chairs. But there is no Wi-Fi facility and there are network issues with the college. The hostel rooms are good and well furnished with water coolers and aqua guards. The food served in the mess is edible.

reviews.review_heading: Looking forward for college to support in extracurricular activities.
reviews.review_text: Wi-Fi facilities are provided in the hostels. Classrooms are well constructed with air-conditioners and proper sitting facilities along with projectors in the lecture halls. The library is big and spacious with all the required books. Labs are big enough to hold approximately 150 students. We have well maintained hostel rooms as well.

reviews.review_heading: Satisfied meals, hostels ect, but excellent in Out Patient Department .
reviews.review_text: Infrastructure is not as satisfied, but library is very much handy, Hostels are satisfactory, meals gives you salivation. There are 2 canteens mainly, common canteen for students and patient and one only for students. Sports are many and inter College competition mainly of CRICKET.

reviews.review_heading: Disappointed with the infrastructure, rules, bad communication, teaching quality & connectivity.
reviews.review_text: The college has no Wi-Fi. Classrooms, labs and campus are very neat and clean. Drinking water is available in the hostel. They give the food 3 times daily. And also they give tea and milk in the evening. For students, medicine is totally free. Sports are not organized every year. But this year a woman cricket match will be organized next month.

review_content.General: Around 120 students got placed in our college. Top roles offered are MD and MS. Students got placed as medical officers in government and civil hospitals. Some students passed foreign exams and got placed in good foreign hospitals. Medical companies visit our college.

individual_reviews.course: MBBS - Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery - Batch of 2025
individual_reviews.title: Good infrastructure and facilities are provided. Hostels are well furnished.

content.Placements: My batch is the 2020 batch, and we will pass out in 2025. It is a 5.5-year course, and each batch has 150 students. After the MBBS internship, the NEET PG exam is conducted for MD. Every year, some students pass the exam and achieve really good ranks, as it is a very tough exam. 100% of students do internships in the same college.
content.Infrastructure: Classrooms are well maintained, and central AC facilities are available. The library also has AC and free Wi-Fi, and it is well spaced. Hostels are well furnished and maintained, with AC facilities, and good quality food is provided. Sports and games facilities are also available.

individual_reviews.course: MBBS - Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery - Batch of 2028
individual_reviews.title: Punjab Institute of Medical Science, Jalandhar .

content.Placements: My college does not provide placements; however, it gives us the opportunity to do an internship for 1 year after the completion of a 4.5-year-long course. Here at the Punjab Institute of Medical Sciences, Jalandhar, after the completion of the course, you have to complete 1 year of necessary internships, which will provide you with a stipend of around Rs 10,000. However, the college does not promise you any placements.
content.Infrastructure: I have opted for a hostel facility in PIMS, Jalandhar. It also has two steel almirahs. And of course, to beat the scorching heat, students are provided with air conditioning, both split and windows; however, there are also rooms available without AC. Talking about the food in PIMS hostels, it is quite good considering that it is hostel food.

faqs.question: How much in-campus salary is offered at Marathwada Mitramandal's College of Pharmacy to M.Pharma students?

fees_and_eligibility.overview: Marathwada Mitramandal's College of Pharmacy offers various programmes at UG and PG levels. Students can choose Full Time courses from stream such as Medicine & Health Sciences. At the UG level, students can choose from courses, namely B.Pharma and D.Pharma. On the other hand, at the PG level, the Marathwada Mitramandal's College of Pharmacy offers M.Pharma. The fees for Marathwada Mitramandal's College of Pharmacy courses range between INR 12,000 to 4,33,044.

faqs.answer: A:    Marathwada Mitramandal's College of Pharmacy is a good institution for D.Pharma, B.Pharma, M.Pharma courses, and these programs are delivered by highly experienced faculty. Major specialisation at Marathwada Mitramandal's College of Pharmacy are Quality Assurance, Pharmaceutical, Pharmacognosy.
faqs.question: Are there any specializations offered by Marathwada Mitramandal's College of Pharmacy?

review_content.Placements: This year, the placement is good. Nearly, about 11 students got selected, where they are provided with a salary of about Rs. 18,000 per month. From our senior batch, about 8 to 9 students got selected. About 30% of the students got placed from college, and thus they offered job having salary about Rs. 10,000 to Rs. 18,000 per month. The companies such as Cipla, Emcure, Advancement, Barclays, etc., and many more visited the campus. These companies provide jobs in the role of a production area, packaging area, QA or QC analyst, or some computer-based work also. About 80% of the student got an internship of one month. It is useful to know what we are studying and what actually happens in the real world.

reviews_list.batch_info: Bachelor of Pharmacy (B.Pharma.) - Batch of 2016
reviews_list.review_title: Faculty are like friends, good education, and no ragging.

review_content.Placements: There is a placement cell in the college. Good placements are done by them. The companies like TCS, Cognizant etc. are coming for the interview. There is what's app group of the college where the placement cell sends the vacancies available in different companies to the passed out batches and help them to switch off the jobs. Also, the increasing numbers of the GPAT qualified students who get the stipend of Rs. 12,400 for M. Pharmacy.
review_content.Infrastructure: Big classrooms, library, labs for every department, Wi-Fi campus, and the medical facilities are also there. On every Tuesday, the doctor comes to visit the college. review_summary.college_name: Marathwada Mitramandal's College of Pharmacy Reviews on Placements, Faculty & Facilities

individual_reviews.course: Diploma in Pharmacy (D.Pharma.) - Batch of 2020
individual_reviews.title: I am satisfied with faculty members, and it gives good placements.

content.Placements: It is the best college, and almost 100% of the students get placed from our college. Our college offers a salary package of around 2 LPA to 4 LPA in our course. Recruiting companies are Sun Pharma, Cipla, etc. Almost 100% of the students got internships from our college. Top roles offered are as a pharmacist and teacher.
content.Infrastructure: The hostel is not bad here. Our college provides various facilities like Wi-Fi, library, classrooms, reading room, computer room, etc. It also provides good quality food in the mess, and the mess was very clean and good. The food was very delicious. Our college arranges sports, and I have enjoyed them very well.

individual_reviews.course: Bachelor of Pharmacy (B.Pharma.) - Batch of 2018
individual_reviews.title: Overall, the college is good due to strict principle and good teachers.

content.Placements: This year, the placement is good. Nearly, about 11 students got selected, where they are provided with a salary of about Rs. 18,000 per month. From our senior batch, about 8 to 9 students got selected. About 30% of the students got placed from college, and thus they offered job having salary about Rs. 10,000 to Rs. 18,000 per month. The companies such as Cipla, Emcure, Advancement, Barclays, etc., and many more visited the campus. These companies provide jobs in the role of a production area, packaging area, QA or QC analyst, or some computer-based work also. About 80% of the student got an internship of one month. It is useful to know what we are studying and what actually happens in the real world.

individual_reviews.course: Bachelor of Pharmacy (B.Pharma.) - Batch of 2016
individual_reviews.title: Faculty are like friends, good education, and no ragging.

content.Placements: There is a placement cell in the college. Good placements are done by them. The companies like TCS, Cognizant etc. are coming for the interview. There is what's app group of the college where the placement cell sends the vacancies available in different companies to the passed out batches and help them to switch off the jobs. Also, the increasing numbers of the GPAT qualified students who get the stipend of Rs. 12,400 for M. Pharmacy.
content.Infrastructure: Big classrooms, library, labs for every department, Wi-Fi campus, and the medical facilities are also there. On every Tuesday, the doctor comes to visit the college. The college also has the volleyball and basketball ground. The college also has the medicinal garden, also has the sports room where the students can play table tennis, carrom, etc.

individual_reviews.course: Bachelor of Pharmacy (B.Pharma.) - Batch of 2019
individual_reviews.title: Worth the money with good placement and qualified of teachers.

content.Placements: The college has great placements. About 10 to 12 companies visit the college every year, and it gives a wide assurance that students get the job. So placement wise, this college is good for those who want to do jobs after their graduation. About 20% of the students get the job through the placements.
content.Infrastructure: The college has a huge infrastructure. Each department has separate labs provided for each subject with all the required and the mandatory instrument. The infrastructure is good and well-maintained. The classrooms are specially designed, and the condition of the classroom is of A1 quality. There is a big library with lots of reference books.

individual_reviews.course: Bachelor of Pharmacy (B.Pharma.) - Batch of 2026
individual_reviews.title: Good teaching but average placements

content.Placements: 60- 70% are placed every year. Haighet is 5 lackhs per anum and lowest is 2 lackhs per anum Top companies include pharma companies Top course offers in our college is B pharmacy ,M pharmacy and PhD as well as diploma in pharmacy. There are faculty members mostly M pharma graduates and phd
content.Infrastructure: Lab facilities are very good but sometimes their is shortage of chemicals .. infrastructure is good but the campus is smaller. Mess food is better or average. Classrooms are well designed with smart interactive boards The labs are good clean and even toilets and washroom are aslo clean and hygeine is maintained.

review_content.General: Our batch joined in 2015 and called The Advayas. This was one of the top dental colleges in Karnataka, affiliated to the Rajiv Gandhi University of Health Sciences, led by Dr. Sadashiva Shetty, the world-renowned orthodontist. There were 85 students placed in the undergraduate course of bachelors in dental surgery through various all India or state competitive exams such as NEET, COMED-K, and KCET. These were merit-based, where students get allotted the seats based on their ranks and scores. I got my seat via KCET, and the package offered by Karnataka Examinations Authority was a basic fee along with an annual tuition fee of Rs 45,000. There was no surplus or donation amount taken. Management seats were a given quota where a certain amount had to be paid to the college for direct admission without appearing in these qualifying exams. The highest salary package offered was 15 LPA. There were compulsory rotatory internships between all 8 departments after the final year i.e. in the fifth year. The students graduate as doctors in this discipline, and the motto that was inculcated in students was dedication, duty, and discipline. Students were taught how to practice dentistry in the face of the global covid-19, and were taught their roles and responsibilities well. Top roles were bachelors degree, master's degree, and placements as a reader or professor in academics of this college. Other roles were as an X-ray or radiology technician, dental technician, and dental assistant. Students can pursue further studies in any specialty of their choice. Students were granted a practicing license by the KSDC, and can be members of the Indian Dental Association to practice anywhere in India, privately or in association with any hospital or organisation.

review_content.Placements: Placements in our college are not that good, but our college has a brand value in India as well as outside India. Many dentists are in eminent positions from BDCH. Any college or dental firm offers recruitment. Top roles offered to the students are professor, assistant professor, intern and clinical assistant.
review_content.Infrastructure: The infrastructure of our college is well-maintained with respect to libraries, classrooms and hygienic clinics. The hostel has a proper mess with decent food services. Sports and games are lacking here, but spaces for a living are awesome to be precise. Washrooms are clean and hygienic.

faqs.answer: Bapuji Dental College admissions are entrance-based. Candidates are selected for BDS based on their NEET score. National Eligibility cum Entrance Test or NEET is conducted once a year in offline or paper-pencil-based test (PBT) mode for three hours and 20 minutes or 200 minutes. For MDS, the selection is based on NEET-MDS score. National Eligibility cum Entrance Test for Masters of Dental Surgery is an entrance exam conducted by NBE in online mode. Besides, admission to PhD course is based on UGC-NET, CSIR-NET or RGUHS Entrance Test score.
faqs.question: Q: Are admissions at Bapuji Dental College and Hospital entrance-based or merit-based?

faqs.answer: Bapuji Dental College offers three courses at UG, PG and doctoral levels. The institute is affiliated with Rajiv Gandhi University of Health Sciences, Bangalore (RGUHS) and is approved by the Dental Council of India (DCI). The college offers a Bachelor of Dental Surgery (BDS) at UG, Master of Dental Surgery (MDS) at PG and PhD at the doctorate level. Admission to all the courses is done based on the score obtained in the entrance exam.
faqs.question: Q: What are the courses offered at Bapuji Dental College?

faqs.answer: Bapuji Dental College and Hospital (BDCH) in Davangere, Karnataka offers 13 courses. The college is known for its undergraduate dental programs

admission_overview.college_name: Bapuji Dental College Admission 2025: Application, Eligibility, Fees & Dates

paragraphs: Bapuji Dental College admissionis entrance-based. The institute considers NEET-UG, NEET MDS and other entrance exam scores for admission to specific courses.Bapuji Dental Collegeoffers UG, PG and doctoral courses in affiliation withRajiv Gandhi University of Health Sciences, Bangalore (RGUHS). BDS and MDS are the flagship programmes of the college. Interested candidates can apply online for the preferred course.

The college considers entrance exam scores and the eligibility of the candidates for admission. Aspirants must fulfil the course-specific eligibility to fill out the application form. Below areBapuji Dental College coursesalong with their eligibility and selection criteria:

The total fee of Bapuji Dental College includes multiple components such as caution deposit, tuition fee, exam fee, etc. Aspirants have to pay a course fee at the time of admission to confirm their seat in the college. Below is the course-wiseBapuji Dental College feeand average package:
Note:This information is sourced from official website/ sanctioning body and is subject to change.

Candidates are offered seats in BDS based on NEET-UG score whereas, NEET MDS is required for MDS programme. Selected candidates have to confirm their seat by paying the course fee. Below is course-wise seat intake for Bapuji Dental College:
Note:This information is sourced from official website/ sanctioning body and is subject to change.

Bapuji Dental College admission processincludes multiple steps such as application, selection, counselling and seat allotment. Aspirants planning to take admission in any of the offered courses must follow all the steps. Below are the steps to get a seat inBapuji Dental College:
STEP 1: Bapuji Dental College Application Process 2025

The college releases the application form online. Aspirants have to apply for NEET for BDS course. For MDS, aspirants first have to fill out the NEET MDS application form. Additionally, aspirants also need to apply on the college website for MDS. However, there is no such clarity for BDS.
STEP 2: Selection

Candidates are selected for BDS programme based on NEET score. The college selects aspirants for MDS based on NEET MDS scores. Besides, aspirants who apply for PhD must pass UGC-NET, CSIR NET or RGUHS Entrance Test. Once theBapuji Dental College cutoffis out, aspirants score equal or above the cutoff score are shortlisted.
STEP 3: Counselling & Seat Allotment

Karnataka Examination Authority conducts counselling and seat allotment for Bapuji Dental College. NEET qualified aspirants have to register on the official website of KEA for BDS and MDS courses. Once the aspirants are offered seat, they need to report for document verification and fee payment.

faqs.question: What is the eligibility for BDS at Bapuji Dental College?
faqs.answer: Aspirants must pass Class 12 with Physics, Chemistry, Biology and English subjects with a minimum of 50% aggregate in Physics, Chemistry and Biology taken together (40% agggregate for SC/ST/Category-I) for getting admission inBDSat Bapuji Dental College and Hospital, Davangere. Besides, aspirants must qualify NEET to apply for admission. Candidates must hold the documents proving their eligibility for verification at the time of admission.

faqs.question: Can I join Bapuji Dental College and Hospital directly?
faqs.answer: Bapuji Dental College does not offer direct admission. Aspirants who wish to get admission in BDS need to qualify NEET. The selection for MDS programme is based on NEET MDS score. Candidates after appearing for the respective exams are considered for admission. The counselling and seat allotment for BDS and MDS courses is done by Karnataka Examination Authority. Besides, the selection for PhD is based on UGC NET, CSIR NET or RGUHS Entrance Test score.

faqs.question: Are admissions at Bapuji Dental College and Hospital entrance-based or merit-based?
faqs.answer: Bapuji Dental College admissions are entrance-based. Candidates are selected for BDS based on their NEET score.National Eligibility cum Entrance Test or NEETis conducted once a year in offline or paper-pencil-based test (PBT) mode for three hours and 20 minutes or 200 minutes. For MDS, the selection is based on NEET-MDS score. National Eligibility cum Entrance Test for Masters of Dental Surgery is an entrance exam conducted by NBE in online mode. Besides, admission to PhD course is based on UGC-NET, CSIR-NET or RGUHS Entrance Test score.

faqs.question: What is the seat intake for Bapuji Dental College?
faqs.answer: Bapuji Dental College has limited seats for BDS and MDS programmes. Aspirants who qualify NEET are offered seat in BDS course. Below is course-wise intake for Bapuji Dental College:CourseSeat IntakeBDS100MDS45Note:This information is sourced from official website/ sanctioning body and is subject to change.

faqs.question: Are admissions open for Bapuji Dental College?
faqs.answer: Yes, Bapuji Dental College admissions are currently open. The application for MDS is available on the college website. Aspirants meeting the eligibility criteria can fill out the form. The selection for BDS and MDS courses is based on NEET and NEET-MDS scores, respectively.

faqs.question: Who are the top recruiters of Bapuji Dental College and Hospital?
faqs.answer: Some top recruiters of Bapuji Dental College and Hospital (BDCH) include: L&T, Tata Motors, Reliance, BHEL, NTPC, Professor, Assistant. BDCH is affiliated with Rajiv Gandhi University of Health Sciences, Karnataka (RGUHS). It offers Bachelor's, Masters, and Doctoral degree programs.

faqs.question: What is the fee for BDS at Bapuji Dental College and Hospital?
faqs.answer: The total tuition fee for Bapuji Dental College BDS is INR 3.8 lakh. This information is sourced from official website/ sanctioning body and is subject to change. Aspirants have to pay the course fee at the time of admission to confirm their seat in the college. Besides, aspirants also have to pay a registration fee to apply for NEET. Candidates who qualify NEET are only considered for BDS admission.

faqs.question: What is the fee structure for a MDS in prosthodontics at Bapuji Dental College and Hospital?
faqs.answer: The fee structure for the Bapuji Dental College and Hospital's MS in Prosthodontics: 11.18 Lacs rupees total for tuition fees. Annual fees collected: 2.5 Lacs for a management seat For a government seat, which requires passing the KCET tests given to students in Karnataka, the costs are much lower.

faqs.question: What is the fee structure for a MS in Conservative Dentistry at Bapuji Dental College and Hospital?
faqs.answer: The Conservative Dentistry & Endodonticsoral MS degree costs INR 320,000 per year. There is a pricing structure for both BDS and MS courses, with MS costing INR 9,60,000. The Bapuji Dental College and Hospital's MS in Conservative Dentistry price structure is INR 1862028 for the private quota and INR 3443100 for the NRI quota. The Conservative Dentistry & Endodonticsoral MS degree has an annual tuition cost of INR 320,000.

overview_faqs.question: Is KIIT School of Public Health a good choice?
overview_faqs.answer: KIIT School of Public Health is one of the prestigious schools which offers top-notch education by experts. The school offers MHA and MPH programmes in full-time mode. The school helps the students to attain good jobs in various sectors such as Hospitals and Healthcare.

overview_faqs.question: What is the scope of healthcare management in India?
overview_faqs.answer: In India, Healthcare/Hospital Sector is in growing face because due to higher no. of population and area we need lot of hospital and best in practice and services. There are lot of institute which provides course in different fields related to Healthcare & Hospital sector. IIHMR Bangalore, for Maharashtra you can look for SGRR Group of Institutions Dehradun.Note: Info is taken from other sources and can be different.

overview_faqs.question: What is the KIIT School of Public Health famous for?
overview_faqs.answer: KIIT School of Public Health is popularly known for its Master's programme in Public Health (MPH) and Hospital Administration (MHA). The school also offers PhD programmes in Public Health. The school offers 2-year programme in full-time mode. Aspiring candidates apply for admission based on merit scores.

admission_process.title: KIIT School Of Public Health - KSPH Admission Process 2025

paragraphs: KIIT School Of Public Health - KSPH admission 2025is conducted in online mode. For offline mode, students must get in touch with the admission office of the institution. The institution offers various courses in the fields ofMedicine and Health Sciences and Business and Management Studies, and more. These programmes are offered atPG and Doctoratelevels. Admission to most of the programmes offered atKIIT School Of Public Health - KSPHare done based on academic merit of the candidates.MPH and MHAare among the popular courses here.
NOTE: Please note that this fee information is obtained from the official website/ sanctioning body but is subject to change.
lists: KIIT School Of Public Health - KSPH Fees: The total tuition fee for PG courses isINR 3,84,000. For Doctorate programmes, the fee isINR 3,00,000. Aspirants need to pay the course fee at the time of admission to confirm their seat in the institution.
KIIT School Of Public Health - KSPH Duration: The institution offers PG programmes for2 yearsduration. For Doctorate level courses, the duration is3 years.

faqs.question: Which entrance exam is accepted at KIIT School Of Public Health MHA?
faqs.answer: In order to qualify for KIIT School Of Public Health MHA admissions, candidates must appear for the KIITEE conducted by KIIT Deemed to be University. The institute does not accept the scores of any other entrance exams to admit students into the MHA course.Candidates must fulfill the eligibility criteria of the MHA course to secure their admission at KIIT School Of Public Health.

faqs.question: Does KIIT School Of Public Health accept NEET-PG scores during admission to MHA?
faqs.answer: No, KIIT School Of Public Health does not accept NEET-PG scores when selecting candidates for the MHA course. Only the scores obtained in the KIITEE will be considered for choosing candidates for the MHA course at KIIT School Of Public Health MHA.Candidates who have a valid NEET-PG score will need to register for the aforementioned KIITEE to apply for KIIT School Of Public Health MHA admissions.

faqs.question: Is KIITEE registration open for KIIT School Of Public Health MHA admissions?
faqs.answer: Yes, the KIITEE registrations for the academic year 2026 are open for KIIT School Of Public Health MHA admissions. Currently, the first phase of KIITEE registrations is ongoing at KIIT Deemed to be University. The last date to apply for the first session of KIITEE 2026 is scheduled on April 8th.

faqs.question: When will the registrations for KIITEE end for KIIT School Of Public Health MHA admissions?
faqs.answer: The final deadline for KIITEE registrations for KIIT School Of Public Health MHA admissions is scheduled on July 1st for the academic year 2026.The aforementioned date is the last date to apply for the third and final round of KIITEE 2026. Candidates who miss this deadline will need to wait for KIITEE 2027 registrations to begin for KIIT School Of Public Health MHA admissions.

faqs.question: Can I get direct admission to the KIIT School of Public Health?
faqs.answer: Yes, students can join directly KIIT School of Public Health. Candidates must qualify the eleigibility criteria which is set by the college for the desired course.KIIT School of Public Health admissionis based on merit scores.

faqs.question: What are the eligibility criteria for admission at KIIT School of Public Health?
faqs.answer: The eligibility criteria for admission at KIIT School of Public Health have been discussed below:·Candidate must have Bachelors’ degree in Medicine, AYUSH,Nursing, Dentistry, Pharmacy, Veterinary Sciences, Physiotherapy, or Allied Health Sciences from a recognised Institute / University with a minimum 50% marks in aggregate (or)·Bachelor’s degree in Technology (BTech),Public Health (BPH),Business Administration (BBA) or Law (LLB) from a recognised Institute / University with minimum 50% marks in aggregate, (or)·Bachelor’s degree in Science / Life Science / Statistics /Economics/ Nutrition / Demography from a recognised Institute / University with a minimum of 50% marks in aggregate, (or)·Post-graduation degree inPsychology, Anthropology or Sociology or Equivalent Degree from a recognised Institute / University with a minimum of 50% marks in aggregate.

faqs.question: Is direct admission possible in MHA at KIIT School Of Public Health?
faqs.answer: No, candidates cannot get direct admission into the KIIT School Of Public Health MHA course. Candidates who want to apply for MHA admissions at KIIT School Of Public Health must appear for the KIITEE first.The selection will be based on the candidate's performance in the aforementioned entrance exam.

faqs.question: Is work experience mandatory for KIIT School Of Public Health MHA admissions?
faqs.answer: No, the KIIT School Of Public Health MHA admissions do not require candidates to have prior experience to be eligible. However, candidates who do have work experience before applying for MHA admissions will be given preference over other candidates.Only work experience in the public health domain will be counted towards KIIT School Of Public Health MHA admissions.

hostel_campus.college_name: KIIT School Of Public Health - KSPH Infrastructure Details & Reviews

hostel.description: There are 20 hostels for boys and 11 hostels for girls are available with accommodation capacity of 10180 and 5648 students respectively. About 30% of the hostel rooms are air-conditioned and all the hostels have attached canteens serving good quality food. Each hostel has 24-hour reading room, the facility for indoor and outdoor games and wireless internet and intranet connectivity. There is a dedicated international hostel accommodates foreign students.
facilities: Class 10th and Class 12th passing certificate as well as marksheet
